blocks.content: The Photon Cannon.
The mass contained within the photons was minuscule, but the rapid rate of photon output created an impact force no ordinary monster could withstand.
The eyes of the Wulke chieftain burned with fury.
Having fought countless battles against humans, he knew exactly how dangerous a mage could be.

blocks.speaker: Martin
blocks.content: That's it! They're scared now! Shirone did it!

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: What they were facing wasn't just a beast—it was a monster.
Even if they had reason, their savagery was nothing to scoff at.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: This isn't over. No matter how strong Shirone is, he's still just human. Beasts that hunt in packs don't retreat just because of one or two casualties.
Just as Altol had predicted, the Wulke chieftain had no intention of backing down.
While the mage's power was destructive, the pack's violence wasn't intimidated by a single individual.
Grrrr, kill… him…
Rooooar!
The Wulke's monstrous roar shook the heavens and earth.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I can do this. If I just keep my focus, I can win.
Shirone teleported into the air and bombarded the ground with his Photon Cannon.
Flashes of light rained down like hail.
Even those not directly hit by the Photon Cannon were struck by the shockwaves erupting from the ground.
Yet, the Wulke's ferocity didn't waver in the slightest.
When gathered in numbers, they were a warlike race that even the ogres—known as the kings of the forest—would yield to.
Ignoring the casualties among their own, the Wulke leaped at Shirone, slashing with their claws.
Shirone hastily landed on the ground, only to find seven more waiting for him.
Shirone transformed his Spirit Zone into an omnidirectional offensive form.
The moment the Spirit Zone's thorns touched the Wulke, a barrage of Photon Cannons fired.
A blinding flash of light erupted, sending the Wulke struck by the photons flying back.

blocks.speaker: Martin
blocks.content: Whoa, that's insane… Shirone.
The children stared in awe.
A human fighting dozens of Wulke on equal footing? They had always thought tales of heroes were just exaggerated rumors.
But watching Shirone fight made even the wildest stories seem believable.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Haa, haa.
Shirone's breathing grew ragged.
Firing the Photon Cannon in rapid succession made him feel like his consciousness might slip away.
Even with the mental fortitude to sustain photon output for over ten minutes, it was nearly unbearable.
The Photon Cannon was fusion magic, combining light and mass.
A mage only had one slot for a spell.
It was the same principle as how humans couldn't focus on two things at once.
Someone like Iruki, who had the Servant Syndrome, could dual-cast, but barring such special cases, even the highest-ranking mages could only use one spell at a time.
Thus, mages who specialized in pure, single-attribute magic were rare in the professional world.
Most combined two, or even three, attributes to maximize the effect of a single spell.
Examples included Siena's Ice Tornado, which combined cold and wind, or Sad's Fire Sun, which fused light and flame.
Fusion magic could maximize efficiency in an instant, but it required immense mental focus.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is bad. I don't think I have many shots left. I doubt I can take them all down.
Wulke were nothing like street thugs.
A beast's keen sense of smell could detect even the slightest hint of fatigue in its prey.
If he showed any sign of weakness, they'd tear him apart in an instant.
True to form, the Wulke chieftain still bared his fangs despite losing a third of his pack.
The Wulke's second wave charged, sensing Shirone's exhaustion.
Their numbers were similar to the first wave, but the feeling was entirely different compared to when Shirone was at full strength.
In desperation, Shirone fired his Photon Cannon.
A mass of photons buried itself in the belly of the lead Wulke.
Rooooar!
The massive body was sent skidding across the ground, but unlike before, it didn't fall.
It dug its legs into the earth and held its ground.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Had my power dropped this much already?
Emboldened, the Wulke chieftain ordered a full assault.
Roar! Roar! Roar!
The Wulke, sensing victory, surged forward.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: In that case…
The only thing left for Shirone was the Immortal Function.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But could he endure it?
This was on a completely different level from when he had used photon output before.
Casting fusion magic—which consumed vast amounts of mental energy—in an infinite domain was uncharted territory.
There was no telling what backlash might follow.
Grrrr!
The moment he activated the Immortal Function, pressure built in Shirone's mind.
It felt like his head might explode.
Aaaah!
Shirone steadied himself and opened his mind.
As his sense of self loosened, an overwhelming power rushed in.
The moment his Spirit Zone filled with light, it exploded violently.
A mass wave sent the Wulke flying, scattering golden fragments everywhere.
Shirone, holding a sphere of light in his palm, unleashed it forward with all his might.
The Photon Cannon shot out as a white flash, devastating the area.
Firing at full power without restraint, the Wulke were swept away like dust under a broom.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But he couldn't maintain this state forever.
If infinity was an ocean, Shirone's mind was a stream.
The Immortal Function was the dam between them.
He was drilling a hole to draw seawater, but if this continued, the dam would collapse.
Different spells required different amounts of mental energy, so mages chose the most cost-effective spell for the situation.
Spells whose effects far outweighed their cost were called Overpower in magical society.
Notable examples included the time magic Stop, the invisibility magic Invisibility, the alchemy magic Injection, and the healing magic Heal of the Almighty.
So, what about the Photon Cannon?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone considered it close to Overpower.
Its exceptional attack speed, versatility between single shots and rapid fire, and even the ability to form beams—it had everything an offensive spell needed.
But it was only efficient relative to its cost.
The Photon Cannon was inherently expensive.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No more! I'll die if this continues!
Shirone stopped casting the Photon Cannon.
The barrage had lasted only about three seconds, but dust filled the air, obscuring everything.
When the wind swept the dust away, the children trembled at the sight before them.
The area swept by the Photon Cannon was utterly devastated.
The Wulke were embedded in the ground as if chewed up, and even those who fled were too terrified to mourn their fallen.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone realized the true power of the Photon Cannon.
Nothing remained where the light had passed.
If even the caster was awed, how much worse must it have been for the targets?
Grrr, we… leave… Let us… go…
The Wulke chieftain conveyed his submission in clumsy human speech.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't ever come back here. If you set foot on this mountain again, it won't end like this.
The Wulke chieftain left first.
The others followed without hesitation, disappearing into the forest.
Only after the Wulke were gone did Shirone's tension ease.
Staggering, he closed his eyes and collapsed.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Shirone! Shirone!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shh! Quiet. They might come back.
Altol's warning made the children cover their mouths.
He then checked on Shirone.
The sight of him sleeping soundly made Altol chuckle.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: The children of the slash-and-burn village had grown over time, but Shirone seemed unchanged, still the same as in his youth.
They say mages don't age.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: At first, he'd thought Shirone was just some mountain hunter's kid—an adopted one at that.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Shirone looked nothing like Uncle Vincent, which made him all the more easy to underestimate.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: But he'd been wrong.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Perhaps Shirone was like the ugly duckling from fairy tales.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: A human fundamentally different from a mountain hunter.
Altol lifted Shirone effortlessly.
Despite his strength, Shirone felt light as a feather, but there was no pride in it.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: He now knew that no matter how light Shirone's body was, the weight of the intellect within it was something he could never lift.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Let's go back. The hunt is over.
Shirone woke with a start, bolting upright.
He was in someone's house.
Placing a hand on the sofa's armrest, he looked around and saw a cozy living room.
Warmth emanated from a fire in the hearth, and the sound of cooking came from the L-shaped kitchen.

blocks.speaker: Martin
blocks.content: Gah!
Shirone's shout was followed by a clatter from the kitchen.
Soon, Rumina peeked out from behind the wall, her face flushed.

blocks.speaker: Rumina
blocks.content: Where…?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then it came back to him. This was Rumina's house, where he'd often stayed with his father as a child.
The children rushed downstairs, still dressed in their hunting gear, as if they'd stayed by Shirone's side instead of going home.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Shirone, are you okay?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Altol asked worriedly.
He'd checked Shirone's condition as soon as they arrived and found nothing wrong.
But his ignorance of magic left room for concern.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone ran a few mental tests to ensure his mind was intact.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: His memories were fine, and his consciousness seemed clear.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm fine. I must've passed out from exhaustion.

blocks.speaker: Martin
blocks.content: Whew, that's a relief. We thought something was seriously wrong.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone felt guilty for worrying them, though the children felt otherwise.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I feel refreshed after a long sleep. The fatigue's finally gone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Despite long rests at the dorm, his exhaustion had never truly lifted.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But now, he felt great.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The persistent sense of dread was gone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He'd been confident he could overcome it.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But now that it was gone, he realized just how hard he'd been pushing himself.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Mr. Armin was right.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: As soon as I mastered the Photon Cannon, my senses returned.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No nightmares, either.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: His stomach growled.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It had been a while since he'd felt this hungry.
The savory aroma from the kitchen only made it worse.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What's cooking?

blocks.speaker: Rumina
blocks.content: Pork stew. Mom bought some meat.
Rumina's mother emerged from the kitchen, ladle in hand.

blocks.speaker: Rumina's mother
blocks.content: Shirone, hello! Long time no see.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hello, ma'am. Sorry for the trouble.

blocks.speaker: Rumina's mother
blocks.content: Oh, don't say that. My daughter's been waiting for you.

blocks.speaker: Rumina
blocks.content: Rumina's face turned bright red.

blocks.speaker: Rumina
blocks.content: But then her expression suddenly saddened, and she bowed her head.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Altol understood.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: He felt the same, and the other children likely did too.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Shirone couldn't be their leader.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: He wasn't one of the slash-and-burn village kids, nor was he the mountain hunter's son.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Shirone was a mage.

blocks.speaker: Altol
blocks.content: Alright, alright! I'm starving. Let's eat first.

blocks.speaker: Martin
blocks.content: Yay! Meat after so long! Meat! Meat! Meat!
Rumina's mother placed a large pot on the table.
The pork stew sloshed, filled to the brim.
